etymology.description: 企(企) (qǐ, "to plan") + 图(圖) (tú, "map; plan; diagram") = the act of planning or plotting something.
etymology.interpretation: 企(企) (qǐ, "to plan") involves a deliberate positioning, standing on tiptoe emblematizes reaching for something, signifying preparation or intent. 图(圖) (tú, "map; plan; diagram") traditionally refers to a drawn plan or project. Together, the characters form the concept of formulating a strategy or making an "attempt" or "intention" to achieve something. The sense of 'intention' derives from the idea of establishing a visual or mental outline of one's goals before acting upon them.

parts.english: to plan; to stand on tiptoe

examples.chinese: 他企图逃跑(他企圖逃跑)。
他们的企图被发现了(他們的企圖被發現了)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen de qǐtú bèi fāxiàn le.

examples.chinese: 即使失败了(即使失敗了)，他仍然不放弃他的企图(他仍然不放棄他的企圖)。
examples.pinyin: Jíshǐ shībàile, tā réngrán bù fàngqì tā de qǐtú.
examples.english: Even if he failed, he still didn't give up his attempt.
